content: Accordion is a vertically stacked set of interactive headings that reveal or hide associated sections of content. Supports controlled and uncontrolled modes. Import: import {Accordion} from '@gravity-ui/uikit';

Accordion props:
- children (React.ReactNode, required): AccordionItem elements to render inside the accordion.
- arrowPosition ('start' | 'end', default 'start'): Position of the expand/collapse arrow icon.
- className (string): CSS class for the root element.
- defaultValue (string | string[]): Initially expanded item(s) for uncontrolled usage.
- disabled (boolean, default false): Disables all accordion items.
- keepMounted (boolean, default false): Keep collapsed item content mounted in the DOM.
- multiple (boolean, default false): Allows multiple items to be expanded simultaneously.
- onUpdate ((value: string | string[]) => void): Callback fired when expanded state changes.
- qa (string): data-qa attribute for testing.
- size ('m' | 'l' | 'xl', default 'm'): Size of accordion items.
- style (React.CSSProperties): Inline styles for the root element.
- value (string | string[]): Controlled expanded item value(s).
- view ('solid' | 'top-bottom', default 'solid'): Visual style of the accordion border.

Basic accordion example:

import {Accordion} from '@gravity-ui/uikit';

<Accordion>
  <Accordion.Item value="item1" title="Section 1">
    Content for section 1
  </Accordion.Item>
  <Accordion.Item value="item2" title="Section 2">
    Content for section 2
  </Accordion.Item>
</Accordion>

Controlled accordion:

const [value, setValue] = React.useState('');

<Accordion value={value} onUpdate={setValue}>
  <Accordion.Item value="a" title="Item A">Content A</Accordion.Item>
  <Accordion.Item value="b" title="Item B">Content B</Accordion.Item>
</Accordion>

ActionTooltip is a tooltip designed for action triggers such as icon buttons. Displays a title, optional description, and optional keyboard shortcut hotkey. Import: import {ActionTooltip} from '@gravity-ui/uikit';

Icon button with action tooltip:

import {ActionTooltip, Button, Icon} from '@gravity-ui/uikit';
import {Gear} from '@gravity-ui/icons';

<ActionTooltip title="Settings" hotkey="Ctrl+,">
  <Button view="flat">
    <Icon data={Gear} />
  </Button>
</ActionTooltip>

ActionTooltip props:
- children (React.ReactElement, required): The trigger element the tooltip is attached to.
- title (string, required): Main label shown in the tooltip.
- closeDelay (number, default 0): Delay in milliseconds before the tooltip closes after the trigger loses hover.
- description (React.ReactNode): Optional descriptive text shown below the title.
- hotkey (string | string[]): Keyboard shortcut displayed at the bottom of the tooltip.
- id (string): ID for the tooltip element.
- openDelay (number, default 500): Delay in milliseconds before the tooltip opens after hover.
- placement (PopupPlacement): Placement of the tooltip relative to the trigger.
- qa (string): data-qa attribute for testing.

ActionsPanel is a floating panel that displays a set of actions, typically used for bulk operations on selected items. Renders at the bottom of a container. Import: import {ActionsPanel} from '@gravity-ui/uikit';

ActionsPanel props:
- actions (ActionsPanelAction[] | ActionsPanelActionGroup[], required): Array of actions or action groups to display in the panel.
- className (string): CSS class for the root element.
- maxRowActions (number): Maximum number of action buttons to show before collapsing into a dropdown.
- onClose (() => void): Callback to close/dismiss the panel.
- qa (string): data-qa attribute for testing.
- renderNote (() => React.ReactNode): Renders additional note content inside the panel.
- style (React.CSSProperties): Inline styles for the root element.

Alert displays an inline notification or feedback message with optional icon, title, message body, and action buttons. Supports multiple themes and visual variants. Import: import {Alert} from '@gravity-ui/uikit';

Info alert with title and message:

import {Alert} from '@gravity-ui/uikit';

<Alert
  theme="info"
  title="Information"
  message="Your session will expire in 5 minutes."
/>

Dismissible danger alert:

<Alert
  theme="danger"
  title="Error"
  message="Failed to save changes."
  onClose={() => setVisible(false)}
/>

Alert props:
- actions (React.ReactNode | AlertAction[]): Action buttons or custom content rendered at the bottom of the alert.
- align ('center' | 'baseline', default 'center'): Vertical alignment of icon and content.
- className (string): CSS class for the root element.
- corners ('rounded' | 'square', default 'rounded'): Controls border radius of the alert.
- icon (React.ReactNode): Custom icon element to display. Overrides the default theme icon.
- layout ('vertical' | 'horizontal', default 'horizontal'): Arrangement of the alert icon and text content.
- message (React.ReactNode): Main body text of the alert.
- onClose (() => void): Callback when the dismiss button is clicked.
- qa (string): data-qa attribute for testing.
- style (React.CSSProperties): Inline styles for the root element.
- theme ('clear' | 'normal' | 'info' | 'success' | 'warning' | 'danger' | 'utility', default 'normal'): Color theme of the alert.
- title (string): Heading text displayed at the top of the alert.
- view ('filled' | 'outlined', default 'filled'): Visual style variant of the alert.

ArrowToggle is a simple animated arrow icon that rotates to indicate an open or closed state, commonly used in dropdowns and expandable sections. Import: import {ArrowToggle} from '@gravity-ui/uikit';

ArrowToggle props:
- className (string): CSS class for the root element.
- direction ('top' | 'left' | 'bottom' | 'right', default 'bottom'): Direction the arrow points.
- size (number, default 16): Size of the arrow icon in pixels.
- style (React.CSSProperties): Inline styles for the root element.
AvatarStack renders a horizontal stack of overlapping Avatar components. Automatically collapses excess avatars into a count indicator or dots. Import: import {AvatarStack} from '@gravity-ui/uikit';

Avatar stack with overflow counter:

import {Avatar, AvatarStack} from '@gravity-ui/uikit';

<AvatarStack max={3} size="m">
  <Avatar text="Alice" />
  <Avatar text="Bob" />
  <Avatar text="Carol" />
  <Avatar text="Dave" />
</AvatarStack>

AvatarStack props:
- children (React.ReactNode, required): Avatar elements to stack.
- className (string): CSS class for the root element.
- max (number): Maximum number of avatars to show before collapsing.
- moreVariant ('counter' | 'dots', default 'counter'): Display style for the overflow indicator.
- overlapSize ('s' | 'm' | 'l', default 'm'): Amount of overlap between adjacent avatars.
- qa (string): data-qa attribute for testing.
- renderMore ((overflowCount: number) => React.ReactNode): Custom renderer for the overflow indicator element.
- size ('3xs' | '2xs' | 'xs' | 's' | 'm' | 'l' | 'xl'): Size passed to all child Avatar components.
- style (React.CSSProperties): Inline styles for the root element.
- total (number): Total count used when computing overflow (defaults to children count).
Avatar displays a user avatar using an image, icon, or initials. Supports multiple sizes, themes, shapes, and can be rendered as a button or link. Import: import {Avatar} from '@gravity-ui/uikit';

Image avatar:

import {Avatar} from '@gravity-ui/uikit';

<Avatar imgUrl="https://example.com/photo.jpg" size="m" alt="Jane Doe" />

Text initials avatar:

<Avatar text="John Smith" size="l" theme="brand" />

Avatar props:
- alt (string): Alt text for the avatar image.
- backgroundColor (string): Custom background color for icon or text avatars.
- borderColor (string): Custom border color for the avatar.
- className (string): CSS class for the root element.
- color (string): Custom icon or text color.
- fallbackImgUrl (string): Fallback image URL when the primary image fails to load.
- icon (SVGIconData): Icon data to render inside the avatar.
- imgUrl (string): URL of the avatar image.
- loading ('eager' | 'lazy'): Image loading behavior.
- qa (string): data-qa attribute for testing.
- shape ('square' | 'circle', default 'circle'): Shape of the avatar.
- size ('3xs' | '2xs' | 'xs' | 's' | 'm' | 'l' | 'xl', default 'm'): Size of the avatar.
- srcSet (string): Responsive image source set.
- style (React.CSSProperties): Inline styles for the root element.
- text (string): Text to display as initials inside the avatar.
- theme ('normal' | 'brand', default 'normal'): Color theme for icon and text avatars.
- title (string): Tooltip text for the avatar element.
- view ('filled' | 'outlined', default 'filled'): Visual style variant.

Breadcrumbs displays a navigation trail showing the user's current location within a hierarchy. Automatically collapses middle items into a popup when there are too many to display. Import: import {Breadcrumbs} from '@gravity-ui/uikit';

Basic breadcrumbs:

import {Breadcrumbs} from '@gravity-ui/uikit';

<Breadcrumbs>
  <Breadcrumbs.Item href="/">Home</Breadcrumbs.Item>
  <Breadcrumbs.Item href="/products">Products</Breadcrumbs.Item>
  <Breadcrumbs.Item>Details</Breadcrumbs.Item>
</Breadcrumbs>

Breadcrumbs props:
- children (React.ReactNode, required): BreadcrumbsItem elements representing each level of the hierarchy.
- className (string): CSS class for the root element.
- disabled (boolean): Disables interaction on all breadcrumb items.
- endContent (React.ReactNode): Content rendered after the last breadcrumb item.
- itemComponent (React.ComponentType): Custom component used to render each breadcrumb item.
- maxItems (number): Maximum number of breadcrumb items to show before collapsing.
- onAction ((key: React.Key) => void): Callback when a breadcrumb item is clicked.
- popupPlacement (PopupPlacement): Placement of the collapsed items popup.
- popupStyle (React.CSSProperties): Inline styles for the overflow popup.
- qa (string): data-qa attribute for testing.
- separator (React.ReactNode): Custom separator element between breadcrumb items.
- showRoot (boolean): Always show the first (root) breadcrumb item even when collapsing.
- style (React.CSSProperties): Inline styles for the root element.
Button is a versatile interactive button component. Supports multiple visual styles, sizes, pin shapes, loading state, and can render as a link. Import: import {Button} from '@gravity-ui/uikit';

Action button:

import {Button} from '@gravity-ui/uikit';

<Button view="action" size="m" onClick={() => console.log('clicked')}>
  Submit
</Button>

Button with icon and loading state:

import {Button, Icon} from '@gravity-ui/uikit';
import {Plus} from '@gravity-ui/icons';

<Button view="outlined" loading={isLoading}>
  <Icon data={Plus} size={16} />
  Add item
</Button>

Button props:
- children (React.ReactNode): Content inside the button, typically text and/or icons.
- className (string): CSS class for the root element.
- component (React.ElementType): Custom component to render as the button root.
- disabled (boolean, default false): Disables the button, preventing interaction.
- extraProps (Record<string, unknown>): Additional HTML attributes passed to the root element.
- href (string): URL to navigate to. When provided, renders an anchor element.
- id (string): id HTML attribute.
- loading (boolean, default false): Shows a loading spinner and disables interaction.
- onClick (React.MouseEventHandler<HTMLElement>): Click event handler.
- pin ('round-round' | 'brick-brick' | 'clear-clear' | 'circle-circle' | 'round-brick' | 'brick-round' | 'round-clear' | 'clear-round' | 'brick-clear' | 'clear-brick' | 'circle-brick' | 'brick-circle' | 'circle-clear' | 'clear-circle', default 'round-round'): Controls the border radius of each side.
- qa (string): data-qa attribute for testing.
- rel (string): rel HTML attribute for link buttons.
- selected (boolean, default false): Marks the button as selected/active.
- size ('xs' | 's' | 'm' | 'l' | 'xl', default 'm'): Size of the button.
- style (React.CSSProperties): Inline styles for the root element.
- target (string): target HTML attribute for link buttons.
- title (string): Native tooltip text.
- type ('button' | 'submit' | 'reset', default 'button'): HTML button type attribute.
- view ('normal' | 'action' | 'outlined' | 'outlined-info' | 'outlined-danger' | 'outlined-warning' | 'outlined-success' | 'outlined-utility' | 'flat' | 'flat-info' | 'flat-danger' | 'flat-warning' | 'flat-success' | 'flat-utility' | 'normal-contrast' | 'outlined-contrast' | 'flat-contrast' | 'raised', default 'normal'): Visual style variant of the button.
- width ('auto' | 'max'): Width behavior.

Card is a container component that groups related content with a visual boundary. Supports selection, action, and container types with various visual styles. Import: import {Card} from '@gravity-ui/uikit';

Container card: import {Card} from '@gravity-ui/uikit';

Card content goes here

 Selectable card: setSelected(!isSelected)} > Click to select

Card props:
- children (React.ReactNode, required): Content to render inside the card.
- className (string): CSS class for the root element.
- disabled (boolean): Disables interaction for selection and action cards.
- onClick (React.MouseEventHandler<HTMLDivElement>): Click handler for action-type cards.
- qa (string): data-qa attribute for testing.
- selected (boolean): Marks the card as selected.
- size ('m' | 'l', default 'm'): Size of the card padding.
- style (React.CSSProperties): Inline styles for the root element.
- theme ('normal' | 'info' | 'success' | 'warning' | 'danger' | 'utility', default 'normal'): Color theme of the card.
- type ('selection' | 'action' | 'container', default 'container'): Semantic type of the card.
- view ('outlined' | 'clear' | 'filled' | 'raised', default 'outlined'): Visual style variant of the card.
Checkbox is a form checkbox input that supports controlled and uncontrolled modes, an indeterminate state, and optional label content. Import: import {Checkbox} from '@gravity-ui/uikit';

Checkbox props:
- checked (boolean): Controlled checked state.
- className (string): CSS class for the root element.
- content (React.ReactNode): Label content rendered next to the checkbox.
- defaultChecked (boolean): Initial checked state for uncontrolled usage.
- disabled (boolean, default false): Disables the checkbox.
- id (string): id HTML attribute for the input element.
- indeterminate (boolean, default false): Renders the checkbox in an indeterminate state.
- onChange (React.ChangeEventHandler<HTMLInputElement>): Native change event handler.
- onUpdate ((checked: boolean) => void): Callback with the new boolean checked value.
- qa (string): data-qa attribute for testing.
- size ('m' | 'l' | 'xl', default 'm'): Size of the checkbox.
- style (React.CSSProperties): Inline styles for the root element.
- title (string): Native tooltip text.
- value (string): Value attribute of the underlying input element.

ClipboardButton is a button that copies a specified text to the clipboard and provides visual feedback via a tooltip that changes on success. Import: import {ClipboardButton} from '@gravity-ui/uikit';

ClipboardButton props:
- text (string, required): The text to copy to the clipboard.
- className (string): CSS class for the root element.
- hasTooltip (boolean, default true): Whether to show a tooltip on hover.
- icon (React.ReactNode): Custom icon element.
- iconPosition ('start' | 'end'): Position of the icon relative to the button content.
- onCopy ((text: string, result: boolean) => void): Callback fired after the copy operation completes.
- qa (string): data-qa attribute for testing.
- size ('xs' | 's' | 'm' | 'l' | 'xl', default 'm'): Size of the button.
- style (React.CSSProperties): Inline styles for the root element.
- timeout (number, default 1200): Duration in milliseconds to show the success state.
- tooltipInitialText (string): Tooltip text shown before copying.
- tooltipSuccessText (string): Tooltip text shown after successful copy.
ClipboardIcon is an icon that animates between clipboard and checkmark states to represent the clipboard copy operation status. Import: import {ClipboardIcon} from '@gravity-ui/uikit';

ClipboardIcon props:
- status ('pending' | 'success' | 'error', required): Current state of the copy operation.
- className (string): CSS class for the root element.
- size (number): Size of the icon in pixels.

CopyToClipboard is a headless component that provides clipboard copy functionality via a render prop pattern. Manages copy status and exposes it to the children function. Import: import {CopyToClipboard} from '@gravity-ui/uikit';

CopyToClipboard props:
- children ((status: 'pending' | 'success' | 'error') => React.ReactElement, required): Render function receiving the current copy status.
- text (string | (() => string), required): Text to copy. Can be a static string or a function that returns a string.
- onCopy ((text: string, result: boolean) => void): Callback fired after the copy operation.
- timeout (number, default 1200): Duration in milliseconds before status resets to pending.

DefinitionList displays a structured list of term-definition pairs. Supports vertical and horizontal layouts, responsive mode, and optional copy-to-clipboard per item. Use DefinitionList.Item for each pair. Import: import {DefinitionList} from '@gravity-ui/uikit';

Horizontal definition list:

import {DefinitionList} from '@gravity-ui/uikit';

<DefinitionList direction="horizontal">
  <DefinitionList.Item name="Name">John Doe</DefinitionList.Item>
  <DefinitionList.Item name="Email" copyText="john@example.com">
    john@example.com
  </DefinitionList.Item>
</DefinitionList>

DefinitionList props:
- children (React.ReactNode, required): DefinitionList.Item elements.
- className (string): CSS class for the root element.
- contentMaxWidth (number): Maximum width in pixels for the definition (value) column.
- direction ('vertical' | 'horizontal', default 'horizontal'): Layout direction of term-definition pairs.
- nameMaxWidth (number): Maximum width in pixels for the term (name) column.
- qa (string): data-qa attribute for testing.
- responsive (boolean): Enables responsive layout that switches direction on small screens.
- style (React.CSSProperties): Inline styles for the root element.

DefinitionList.Item props:
- name (React.ReactNode, required): The term label.
- children (React.ReactNode): The definition value.
- copyText (string): Text to copy when clicked.
- note (React.ReactNode): Additional note shown below the value.

Dialog is a modal dialog with a structured layout of header, body, and footer sections. Built on top of Modal with a close button and standard slot components: Dialog.Header, Dialog.Body, Dialog.Footer. Import: import {Dialog} from '@gravity-ui/uikit';

Confirmation dialog:

import {Dialog, Button} from '@gravity-ui/uikit';

<Dialog open={open} onClose={() => setOpen(false)} size="s">
  <Dialog.Header caption="Confirm action" />
  <Dialog.Body>Are you sure you want to delete this item?</Dialog.Body>
  <Dialog.Footer
    onClickButtonApply={() => { handleDelete(); setOpen(false); }}
    onClickButtonCancel={() => setOpen(false)}
    textButtonApply="Delete"
    textButtonCancel="Cancel"
  />
</Dialog>

Dialog props:
- children (React.ReactNode, required): Dialog content — typically Dialog.Header, Dialog.Body, and Dialog.Footer.
- onClose (() => void, required): Callback to close the dialog.
- open (boolean, required): Controls dialog visibility.
- className (string): CSS class for the root element.
- contentOverflow ('visible' | 'auto', default 'visible'): Controls scrolling behavior inside the dialog.
- disableBodyScrollLock (boolean, default false): Disables the body scroll lock when dialog is open.
- disableEscapeKeyDown (boolean, default false): Prevents closing the dialog on Escape key press.
- disableOutsideClick (boolean, default false): Prevents closing the dialog when clicking outside.
- hasCloseButton (boolean, default true): Shows a close button in the top-right corner.
- keepMounted (boolean, default false): Keep the dialog mounted in the DOM when closed.
- qa (string): data-qa attribute for testing.
- size ('s' | 'm' | 'l'): Width preset for the dialog.
- style (React.CSSProperties): Inline styles for the root element.
Disclosure is a collapsible section with a summary trigger and expandable content area. Supports controlled and uncontrolled modes. Import: import {Disclosure} from '@gravity-ui/uikit';

Basic disclosure:

import {Disclosure} from '@gravity-ui/uikit';

<Disclosure summary="Show more details">
  Here is the expanded content that was hidden.
</Disclosure>

Disclosure props:
- arrowPosition ('left' | 'right' | 'start' | 'end', default 'start'): Position of the expand arrow icon.
- className (string): CSS class for the root element.
- defaultExpanded (boolean, default false): Initial expanded state for uncontrolled usage.
- disabled (boolean, default false): Disables the toggle interaction.
- expanded (boolean): Controlled expanded state.
- keepMounted (boolean, default true): Keep content mounted when collapsed.
- onSummaryKeyDown (React.KeyboardEventHandler): Key down event handler on the summary element.
- onUpdate ((expanded: boolean) => void): Callback fired when the expanded state changes.
- qa (string): data-qa attribute for testing.
- size ('m' | 'l' | 'xl', default 'm'): Size of the disclosure trigger.
- style (React.CSSProperties): Inline styles for the root element.
- summary (React.ReactNode): Content for the always-visible trigger area.
Divider is a thin horizontal or vertical line used to separate sections of content. Supports an optional label and alignment. Import: import {Divider} from '@gravity-ui/uikit';

Horizontal divider with label:

import {Divider} from '@gravity-ui/uikit';

<Divider align="center">OR</Divider>

Vertical divider:

<Divider orientation="vertical" />

Divider props:
- align ('start' | 'center' | 'end', default 'start'): Alignment of the optional label within the divider.
- children (React.ReactNode): Optional label text or element displayed within the divider line.
- className (string): CSS class for the root element.
- orientation ('vertical' | 'horizontal', default 'horizontal'): Orientation of the divider line.
- qa (string): data-qa attribute for testing.
- style (React.CSSProperties): Inline styles for the root element.

Drawer is a sliding panel that appears from the edge of the screen. Supports left, right, top, and bottom placement, optional resizing, and backdrop dismissal. Import: import {Drawer} from '@gravity-ui/uikit';

Right side drawer: import {Drawer, Button} from '@gravity-ui/uikit';

Settings

Drawer props:
- open (boolean, required): Controls drawer visibility.
- className (string): CSS class for the root element.
- hideVeil (boolean, default false): Hides the backdrop overlay behind the drawer.
- maxSize (number): Maximum size in pixels when resizable.
- minSize (number): Minimum size in pixels when resizable.
- onOpenChange ((open: boolean) => void): Callback when the open state changes.
- onResizeComplete ((size: number) => void): Callback fired when a resize drag operation completes.
- onResizeStart (() => void): Callback fired when a resize drag operation starts.
- placement ('left' | 'right' | 'top' | 'bottom', default 'left'): Edge from which the drawer slides in.
- qa (string): data-qa attribute for testing.
- resizable (boolean, default false): Enables drag-to-resize functionality.
- size (number | 'auto'): Initial size of the drawer in pixels or auto.
- style (React.CSSProperties): Inline styles for the root element.

DropdownMenu is a button that opens a dropdown menu with a list of actions. Supports nested submenus, icons, disabled items, and a custom switcher trigger. Import: import {DropdownMenu} from '@gravity-ui/uikit';

DropdownMenu props:
- children (React.ReactNode): Alternative to items — use DropdownMenu.Item elements as children.
- data (unknown): Extra data passed to item handler callbacks.
- disabled (boolean): Disables the menu trigger button.
- hideOnScroll (boolean, default true): Closes the menu when the page is scrolled.
- icon (React.ReactNode): Icon for the default ellipsis trigger button.
- items (DropdownMenuItem[] | DropdownMenuItem[][]): Menu items array. Use nested arrays to create groups separated by dividers.
- onOpenToggle ((open: boolean) => void): Callback when the open state changes.
- open (boolean): Controlled open state of the menu.
- qa (string): data-qa attribute for testing.
- renderSwitcher ((props: DropdownMenuSwitcherProps) => React.ReactNode): Custom render function for the trigger button.
- size ('s' | 'm' | 'l' | 'xl', default 'm'): Size of the trigger button and menu items.
- style (React.CSSProperties): Inline styles for the root element.

DropdownMenuItem shape:
- text (string): Item label.
- action ((event, data) => void): Click handler.
- disabled (boolean): Disables the item.
- hidden (boolean): Hides the item.
- theme ('normal' | 'danger'): Item color theme.
- icon (React.ReactNode): Icon displayed before the text.
- items (DropdownMenuItem[]): Nested submenu items.

FilePreview displays a preview tile for a file with its name, type icon or image thumbnail, and optional action buttons. Used in file upload UIs. Import: import {FilePreview} from '@gravity-ui/uikit';

FilePreview props:
- file (File, required): The File object representing the file to preview.
- actions (FilePreviewAction[]): Action buttons shown on hover over the preview tile.
- className (string): CSS class for the root element.
- description (string): Additional descriptive text shown below the file name.
- imageSrc (string): URL of an image to show as a thumbnail preview.
- onClick ((file: File) => void): Callback when the preview tile is clicked.
- qa (string): data-qa attribute for testing.
- selected (boolean): Marks the preview as selected.
- style (React.CSSProperties): Inline styles for the root element.
- view ('default' | 'compact', default 'default'): Size variant of the preview tile.
HelpMark is a small question mark button that shows a popover with help content when clicked or hovered. Used for inline contextual help. Import: import {HelpMark} from '@gravity-ui/uikit';

Help mark with popover content:

import {HelpMark, Text} from '@gravity-ui/uikit';

<HelpMark>
  This field requires a valid email address.
</HelpMark>

HelpMark props:
- children (React.ReactNode): Content displayed inside the popover.
- buttonProps (React.ButtonHTMLAttributes<HTMLButtonElement>): Additional HTML attributes for the trigger button.
- className (string): CSS class for the root element.
- iconSize ('s' | 'm' | 'l' | 'xl', default 'm'): Size of the question mark icon: s=14px, m=16px, l=18px, xl=20px.
- popoverProps (Partial<PopoverProps>): Props passed through to the underlying Popover component.
- qa (string): data-qa attribute for testing.
- style (React.CSSProperties): Inline styles for the root element.
Hotkey renders a keyboard shortcut visually as individual key badges. Handles platform-specific key symbols for Mac and PC. Import: import {Hotkey} from '@gravity-ui/uikit';

Hotkey props:
- value (string, required): Keyboard shortcut string, e.g. 'mod+k' or 'ctrl+shift+p'.
- className (string): CSS class for the root element.
- platform ('pc' | 'mac'): Platform for key symbol rendering. Defaults to auto-detect from user agent.
- style (React.CSSProperties): Inline styles for the root element.
- view ('light' | 'dark', default 'light'): Visual theme of the key badges.

Icon renders an SVG icon from the Gravity UI icon set. Accepts icon data and supports size, color, and fill/stroke overrides. Import: import {Icon} from '@gravity-ui/uikit'; import {Gear} from '@gravity-ui/icons';

Icon props:
- data (SVGIconData, required): SVG icon data imported from @gravity-ui/icons or a custom SVG component.
- className (string): CSS class for the root element.
- color (string): CSS color value applied to the icon.
- fill (string, default 'currentColor'): SVG fill attribute override.
- height (number | string): Explicit height override.
- qa (string): data-qa attribute for testing.
- size (number): Sets both width and height of the icon in pixels.
- stroke (string, default 'none'): SVG stroke attribute override.
- style (React.CSSProperties): Inline styles for the root element.
- width (number | string): Explicit width override.
Label is a small tag or badge component used to display metadata, statuses, or categories. Supports copy, close, and info interaction types. Import: import {Label} from '@gravity-ui/uikit';

Status label:

import {Label} from '@gravity-ui/uikit';

<Label theme="success">Active</Label>

Key-value label with copy:

<Label type="copy" copyText="abc123" value="abc123">ID</Label>

Label props:
- children (React.ReactNode): Label text content.
- className (string): CSS class for the root element.
- copyText (string): Text to copy when type is 'copy'.
- disabled (boolean): Disables interaction.
- icon (React.ReactNode): Icon element to display at the start of the label.
- interactive (boolean): Makes the label appear clickable even when no onClick is provided.
- loading (boolean, default false): Shows a loading spinner inside the label.
- onClick (React.MouseEventHandler<HTMLElement>): Click handler for the label body.
- onCloseClick (React.MouseEventHandler<HTMLElement>): Click handler for the close button when type is 'close'.
- onCopy ((text: string, result: boolean) => void): Callback after copy when type is 'copy'.
- qa (string): data-qa attribute for testing.
- size ('xxs' | 'xs' | 's' | 'm', default 'xs'): Size of the label.
- style (React.CSSProperties): Inline styles for the root element.
- theme ('normal' | 'info' | 'danger' | 'warning' | 'success' | 'utility' | 'unknown' | 'clear', default 'normal'): Color theme of the label.
- type ('default' | 'copy' | 'close' | 'info', default 'default'): Interaction type of the label.
- value (string): Secondary text value shown after a colon separator.
- width ('auto'): Width behavior of the label.
Link is a styled anchor element for navigation. Supports multiple visual variants and optional visited state styling. Import: import {Link} from '@gravity-ui/uikit';

External link:

import {Link} from '@gravity-ui/uikit';

<Link href="https://example.com" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">
  Visit Example
</Link>

Link props:
- href (string, required): URL the link navigates to.
- children (React.ReactNode): Link text or content.
- className (string): CSS class for the root element.
- onClick (React.MouseEventHandler<HTMLAnchorElement>): Click event handler.
- qa (string): data-qa attribute for testing.
- rel (string): rel HTML attribute.
- style (React.CSSProperties): Inline styles for the root element.
- target (string): target HTML attribute.
- title (string): Native tooltip text.
- underline (boolean, default false): Shows underline on the link text.
- view ('normal' | 'primary' | 'secondary', default 'normal'): Visual style variant of the link.
- visitable (boolean, default false): Enables visited state styling.

List is a performant list component supporting virtual scrolling, filtering, sorting, and custom item rendering. Used for long lists and select-like interfaces. Import: import {List} from '@gravity-ui/uikit';

List props:
- items (T[], required): Array of items to render in the list.
- activeItemIndex (number): Index of the keyboard-focused item.
- className (string): CSS class for the root element.
- deactivateOnLeave (boolean): Clears the active item when the mouse leaves the list.
- emptyPlaceholder (React.ReactNode): Content shown when items array is empty or filter returns no results.
- filter (string): Controlled filter value.
- filterItem ((filter: string, item: T) => boolean): Custom predicate for filtering items.
- filterPlaceholder (string): Placeholder text for the filter input.
- filterable (boolean): Enables a built-in text filter input above the list.
- id (string): id HTML attribute.
- itemClassName (string): CSS class applied to each item wrapper.
- itemHeight (number | ((item: T) => number)): Height of each item in pixels. Required for virtualization.
- itemsClassName (string): CSS class for the items container.
- itemsHeight (number | ((items: T[]) => number)): Total height of the items container.
- loading (boolean): Shows a loading indicator.
- onChangeActive ((index: number | undefined) => void): Callback when the active item index changes.
- onItemClick ((item: T, index: number) => void): Callback when an item is clicked.
- onLoadMore (() => void): Callback fired when scrolled near the bottom for infinite loading.
- onSortEnd (({oldIndex, newIndex}) => void): Callback after drag-to-sort completes.
- qa (string): data-qa attribute for testing.
- renderItem ((item: T, isActive: boolean, itemIndex: number) => React.ReactNode): Custom renderer for each list item.
- role (string): ARIA role for the list element.
- selectedItemIndex (number | number[]): Index or indices of selected items.
- sortHandleAlign ('left' | 'right'): Alignment of the drag sort handle.
- sortable (boolean): Enables drag-to-reorder functionality.
- virtualized (boolean): Enables virtual scrolling for large lists.
Loader is an animated spinner used to indicate a loading state. Comes in three sizes. Import: import {Loader} from '@gravity-ui/uikit';

Loader props:
- className (string): CSS class for the root element.
- qa (string): data-qa attribute for testing.
- size ('s' | 'm' | 'l', default 's'): Size of the spinner.
- style (React.CSSProperties): Inline styles for the root element.
Menu is a vertical list of menu items with optional grouping. Provides Menu.Item for individual actions and Menu.Group for labeled sections. Import: import {Menu} from '@gravity-ui/uikit';

Menu with groups:

import {Menu} from '@gravity-ui/uikit';

<Menu>
  <Menu.Item onClick={() => handleEdit()}>Edit</Menu.Item>
  <Menu.Group label="Danger Zone">
    <Menu.Item theme="danger" onClick={() => handleDelete()}>Delete</Menu.Item>
  </Menu.Group>
</Menu>

Menu props:
- children (React.ReactNode): Menu.Item and Menu.Group elements.
- className (string): CSS class for the root element.
- qa (string): data-qa attribute for testing.
- size ('s' | 'm' | 'l' | 'xl', default 'm'): Size of the menu and its items.
- style (React.CSSProperties): Inline styles for the root element.

Menu.Item props:
- children (React.ReactNode): Item label content.
- active (boolean, default false): Marks the item as active.
- contentClassName (string): CSS class for the content element.
- disabled (boolean, default false): Disables the item.
- extraProps (Record): Additional HTML attributes.
- href (string): URL for link items.
- iconEnd (React.ReactNode): Icon displayed after the item text.
- iconStart (React.ReactNode): Icon displayed before the item text.
- onClick (React.MouseEventHandler): Click event handler.
- qa (string): data-qa attribute for testing.
- rel (string): rel HTML attribute.
- selected (boolean, default false): Marks the item as selected.
- target (string): target HTML attribute.
- theme ('normal' | 'danger', default 'normal'): Color theme of the item.
- title (string): Title HTML attribute.

Menu.Group props:
- children (React.ReactNode): Menu items in this group.
- label (string): Group header label.
- qa (string): data-qa attribute for testing.

Modal is a base modal overlay component that renders content above the page with a backdrop. Manages focus trapping, scroll locking, and dismiss interactions. Use Dialog for structured modal dialogs. Import: import {Modal} from '@gravity-ui/uikit';

Basic modal: import {Modal, Button} from '@gravity-ui/uikit'; const [open, setOpen] = React.useState(false); <>

Modal content

Modal props:
- children (React.ReactNode): Content rendered inside the modal.
- className (string): CSS class for the overlay element.
- container (HTMLElement): DOM element to mount the modal via Portal. Defaults to document.body.
- contentClassName (string): CSS class for the inner content element.
- contentOverflow ('visible' | 'auto', default 'visible'): Controls scroll behavior inside the modal content.
- disableBodyScrollLock (boolean, default false): Disables locking page scroll while the modal is open.
- disableEscapeKeyDown (boolean, default false): Prevents dismissal on Escape key press.
- disableHeightTransition (boolean, default false): Disables animated height transitions.
- disableOutsideClick (boolean, default false): Prevents dismissal on clicks outside the modal content.
- disablePortal (boolean, default false): Renders modal inline instead of via a Portal.
- disableVisuallyHiddenDismiss (boolean): Suppresses the visually hidden accessibility dismiss buttons.
- floatingRef (React.RefObject<HTMLDivElement>): Ref attached to the floating content element.
- initialFocus (number | React.MutableRefObject<HTMLElement | null>): Element or index to focus initially when the modal opens.
- keepMounted (boolean, default false): Keep the modal DOM tree mounted when closed.
- onClose ((event, reason) => void, deprecated): Deprecated: use onOpenChange instead.
- onEnterKeyDown ((event: KeyboardEvent) => void, deprecated): Deprecated callback for Enter key press.
- onEscapeKeyDown ((event: KeyboardEvent) => void, deprecated): Deprecated: use onOpenChange instead.
- onOpenChange ((open: boolean, event?, reason?) => void): Callback when the open state changes.
- onOutsideClick ((event: MouseEvent) => void, deprecated): Deprecated: use onOpenChange instead.
- onTransitionIn (() => void): Callback when the open transition starts.
- onTransitionInComplete (() => void): Callback when the open transition completes.
- onTransitionOut (() => void): Callback when the close transition starts.
- onTransitionOutComplete (() => void): Callback when the close transition completes.
- open (boolean, default false): Controls modal visibility.
- qa (string): data-qa attribute for testing.
- returnFocus (boolean | React.MutableRefObject<HTMLElement | null>): Element to return focus to when modal closes.
- style (React.CSSProperties): Inline styles for the overlay element.
